title: mentha × villosa var. alopecuroides
intro: Mentha × villosa var. alopecuroides, also known as Woolly Mint, is a hybrid plant within the mint family (Lamiaceae), characterized by its woolly, downy leaves and stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems. The health benefits of Woolly Mint include its potential to ease nausea, reduce fever, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for stress relief and digestive issues. Woolly Mint is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
